{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query specifically asks about anticoagulants used for blood glucose testing in primary health care (PHC). Reviewing the documents, Document [3] mentions that potassium oxalate is used with sodium fluoride to prevent late clotting and stabilize glucose levels for up to 3 days. Document [9] confirms that potassium oxalate tubes contain sodium fluoride and are used for plasma glucose and lactic acid testing. Therefore, the combination of potassium oxalate and sodium fluoride is the appropriate anticoagulant for blood glucose testing in PHC.", "answer_choice": "C"}
